blocked_on: fetch annexed raw payloads before running signal-level baselines
implement and validate the event-based alignment and drift-handling procedure on real payloads
integrate the validated SNIRF-to-HbO/HbR derivation path into canonical waveform ingest
define how variable fNIRS montages and sampling rates will be normalized across subjects

notes: Candidate public paired dataset for EEG-fNIRS latent alignment.
Based on verified DOI 10.18112/openneuro.ds004514.v1.1.2 checked on March 21, 2026.
Source-backed intake confirmed 12 subject folders at the frozen 1.1.2 snapshot with no session subdirectories.
Dataset README and BIDS sidecars confirm 64 EEG channels at 2048 Hz plus auxiliary channels.
Sub-01 inspection shows matched EEG and fNIRS event sequences with non-identical absolute onsets, so downstream pairing should be event-aligned rather than naive timestamp-matched.
The subject split policy and canonical normalization/alignment rules are documented in docs/cross-modal/ds004514-split-plan.md and docs/cross-modal/ds004514-normalization-note.md.
Waveform-level fNIRS smoke processing on real SNIRF payloads found subject-specific raw variants with 22 or 28 channels and sampling rates of 7.8125 Hz or 8.928571 Hz.
SNIRF loading, optical-density conversion, HbO/HbR derivation, and scalp-coupling scoring have been validated on the raw fNIRS payloads.
